title_en: Siyar and International Law in Al-Mabsut
content_en:
One of the most distinctive features of Al-Mabsut is its comprehensive treatment of siyar — the Islamic law governing relations between the Muslim state and non-Muslim polities, covering the law of jihad, treaties, the status of non-Muslim subjects, and the rules for conduct in warfare. As-Sarakhsi's coverage of siyar in Al-Mabsut is the most comprehensive in the classical Hanafi tradition and establishes the school's positions on what is effectively a system of international law.

The siyar sections of Al-Mabsut begin with as-Sarakhsi's commentary on Muhammad ash-Shaybani's As-Siyar al-Kabir — the foundational Hanafi text on the law of nations, which ash-Shaybani produced as one of his major works. As-Sarakhsi's commentary on As-Siyar al-Kabir within Al-Mabsut is in itself a major scholarly work, expanding ash-Shaybani's terse rulings with the evidential basis, comparative analysis, and case-by-case reasoning that make the principles applicable to specific circumstances.

The foundational concept of siyar in Al-Mabsut is the division of the world into Dar al-Islam (the realm of Islam, under Islamic governance) and Dar al-Harb (the realm of war, under non-Islamic governance). As-Sarakhsi discusses the legal implications of this division: the duties of Muslims in each realm, the legal status of contracts made across the boundary, the treatment of combatants and non-combatants in warfare, and the conditions for valid peace treaties.

The rules for the treatment of non-Muslim subjects (ahl adh-dhimmah) in Al-Mabsut reflect the Hanafi school's developed framework for managing religious plurality within the Islamic polity. As-Sarakhsi addresses the obligations of dhimmi communities (payment of the jizya, observance of certain public restrictions) and their corresponding rights (protection of life, property, places of worship, and the right to follow their own religious laws in personal matters). His analysis reflects the Hanafi school's relatively developed approach to religious coexistence within an Islamic political framework.

The law of amān (safe conduct) is addressed in Al-Mabsut with characteristic Hanafi detail. Any Muslim may grant safe conduct to a non-Muslim, and the grant is binding on the entire Muslim community — a principle of considerable importance in commercial and diplomatic relations between Muslim and non-Muslim states. As-Sarakhsi discusses the conditions for valid amān, its duration, and the consequences of violating it.

The treatment of warfare in Al-Mabsut addresses the rules governing the conduct of military operations: the prohibition on killing non-combatants (women, children, the elderly, religious personnel, farmers not engaged in combat), the treatment of captives, the distribution of war spoils, and the conditions for valid peace negotiations. As-Sarakhsi's analysis reflects the Hanafi school's comprehensive engagement with the ethics and law of warfare — a tradition that anticipates some features of modern international humanitarian law.

Al-Mabsut's siyar sections represent a unique contribution to the Islamic legal tradition and to the history of international law more broadly. Scholars of Islamic international law cite As-Siyar al-Kabir and Al-Mabsut as foundational texts, and as-Sarakhsi's comprehensive analysis continues to be referenced in contemporary discussions of Islamic positions on international relations, warfare, and inter-communal life.
title_id: Siyar dan Hukum Internasional dalam Al-Mabsut
content_id:
Salah satu fitur paling khas dari Al-Mabsut adalah pembahasan komprehensifnya tentang siyar — hukum Islam yang mengatur hubungan antara negara Muslim dan negara-negara non-Muslim, mencakup hukum jihad, perjanjian, status warga non-Muslim di bawah pemerintahan Islam (dhimmi), dan hukum yang berlaku dalam kondisi perang dan damai.

Bagian siyar dalam Al-Mabsut as-Sarakhsi terinspirasi terutama oleh Kitab al-Siyar al-Kabir karya Muhammad ibn al-Hasan asy-Syaibani, yang merupakan salah satu karya paling awal dalam hukum internasional Islam. As-Sarakhsi kemudian menulis syarah tersendiri atas kitab tersebut, namun pembahasannya dalam Al-Mabsut memberikan versi yang lebih terstruktur dan lebih mudah diakses.

As-Sarakhsi mendefinisikan jihad sebagai kewajiban kolektif (fardhu kifayah) yang dapat menjadi kewajiban individual (fardhu 'ain) dalam kondisi tertentu. Beliau membahas secara rinci syarat-syarat sahnya jihad, siapa yang wajib berpartisipasi, bagaimana memperlakukan tawanan perang, dan bagaimana membagi ghanimah (rampasan perang). Seluruh pembahasan ini dilakukan dalam kerangka hukum yang ketat, bukan retorika politik atau keagamaan.

Pembahasan tentang perjanjian ('ahd) dan gencatan senjata (hudnah) sangat relevan dari perspektif hukum internasional kontemporer. As-Sarakhsi menganalisis kondisi-kondisi di mana perjanjian dapat dibuat, berapa lama perjanjian dapat berlaku, dan apa konsekuensi hukumnya jika salah satu pihak melanggar perjanjian. Prinsip-prinsip yang beliau rumuskan mendahului banyak konsep yang kemudian berkembang dalam hukum internasional modern.

Bagian tentang status dhimmi (warga non-Muslim di bawah pemerintahan Islam) membahas hak-hak dan kewajiban mereka, pajak jizyah, dan batasan-batasan yang dikenakan kepada mereka. Pandangan as-Sarakhsi mencerminkan pendekatan Hanafi yang relatif praktis dan akomodatif terhadap pluralisme agama dalam konteks pemerintahan Islam.
content_ar:
من أبرز خصائص المبسوط معالجته الشاملة لأبواب السِّيَر — أي أحكام علاقة الدولة الإسلامية بالدول غير الإسلامية — وتشمل: أحكام الجهاد والمعاهدات ووضع أهل الذمة وآداب القتال. ويُعالج السرخسي هذه الأبواب بالعمق الاستثنائي نفسه الذي يُعالج به سائر أبواب الفقه.

وأبواب السِّيَر في المبسوط تستند في مضمونها إلى كتاب السِّيَر الكبير لمحمد بن الحسن الشيباني (وشرحه للسرخسي)، وتُقدِّم نسخة موجزة منه مع إضافات تحليلية. وهذا التقاطع بين المبسوط وكتاب السِّيَر الكبير يجعل أبواب السِّيَر في المبسوط أكثر غنىً مما يتوقعه القارئ في عمل موسوعي شامل.

ومسائل الجهاد في المبسوط تُعالَج بدقة أصولية عالية؛ فيُبيِّن السرخسي شروط فرضيته وأحكام الإذن في الخروج إليه وآداب القتال وأحكام الغنائم والأسرى. ومسائل عقود الأمان وآثارها من أكثر المسائل التي يبدو فيها التطور الحنفي في تصور العلاقات الدولية.

وأحكام أهل الذمة في المبسوط وافية الاستيعاب؛ ويتناول السرخسي حقوقهم وواجباتهم وشروط عقد الذمة والمسائل المتعلقة بوضعهم القانوني في الدولة الإسلامية. وهذه الأبواب لها قيمة خاصة للباحث في تاريخ القانون الإسلامي الدولي الكلاسيكي. وأبواب المعاهدات والهدنات في المبسوط تتضمن أحكام ابتداء المعاهدة وشروط صحتها ومدتها وأحكام نقضها.
